LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ARMAN JAVID'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING EXCEPT FOR THE SENTENCE RE: DECLARATION BY DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL, MARC MAZER. BEFORE FILING ANY DEMURRER, COUNSEL "SHALL MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE." (CCP 430.41(A) (EMPHASIS ADDED). COUNSEL'S DECLARATION INDICATES THAT DID NOT OCCUR HERE, SO THE DEMURRER AND ITS ASSOCIATED MOTION TO STRIKE ARE ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. MOREOVER AND IN ANY EVENT, LEAVE TO AMEND IS LIBERALLY PERMITTED BY CALIFORNIA'S COURTS, SO I GRANT PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. (SEE CCP 473(A), 576; HIRSA V. SUP. CT. (1981) 118 CAL.APP.3D 486, 490.) SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED BY 10/26/18. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B.ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JEFFREY LINDEN'S DEMURRER TO 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT JEFFREY LINDEN'S DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR UNJUST ENRICHMENT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. "'[A]N ACTION BASED ON AN IMPLIED-IN-FACT OR QUASI-CONTRACT CANNOT LIE WHERE THERE EXISTS BETWEEN THE PARTIES A VALID EXPRESS CONTRACT COVERING THE SAME SUBJECT MATTER.' [CITATION.] HOWEVER, 'RESTITUTION MAY BE AWARDED IN LIEU OF BREACH OF CONTRACT DAMAGES WHEN THE PARTIES HAD AN EXPRESS CONTRACT, BUT IT WAS PROCURED BY FRAUD OR IS UNENFORCEABLE OR INEFFECTIVE FOR SOME REASON.' [CITATION.] THUS, A PARTY TO AN EXPRESS CONTRACT CAN ASSERT A CLAIM FOR RESTITUTION BY 'ALLEG[ING IN THAT CAUSE OF ACTION] THAT THE EXPRESS CONTRACT IS VOID OR WAS RESCINDED.'" (RUTHERFORD HOLDINGS, LLC V. PLAZA DEL REY (2014) 223 CAL.APP.4TH 221, 231.) (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)A PROPOSED ORDER IS SUBMITTED BY EMAIL. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC., MISHA BREYBURG, SASHA PLOTITSA'S MOTION TO STRIKE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT [CCP 436]. DEFENDANTS MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC., MISHA BREYBURG, AND SASHA PLOTITSA'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT, JOINED BY DEFENDANT JEFFREY LINDEN, IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF'S FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT TECHNICALLY WAS LATE-FILED, THE COURT'S APRIL 23, 2019 ORDER DID NOT SPECIFY A DEADLINE FOR PLAINTIFF TO FILE A FURTHER AMENDED COMPLAINT, AND DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN THEY WERE PREJUDICED BY THE 15-DAY DELAY. HERE, AS IN HARLAN V. DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION (2005) 132 CAL.APP.4TH 868, "'THE DELAY IN FILING WAS BRIEF AND INCONSEQUENTIAL.'" (ID. AT 872-874 [TRIAL COURT HAD DISCRETION TO ACCEPT LATE-FILED SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHOUT REQUIRING MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND].) DEFENDANTS' ALTERNATIVE REQUEST THAT THE COURT CONDITION LEAVE TO AMEND ON PAYMENT OF DEFENDANTS' FEES FOR FILING THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REMINDED TO SUBMIT COURTESY COPIES OF ALL OF HIS FILED AND LODGED PAPERS WITH DEPARTMENT 302 AS REQUIRED BY LOCAL RULE 2.7(B). COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. BEFORE FILING ANY DEMURRER, COUNSEL "SHALL MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE." (CCP 430.41(A) (EMPHASIS ADDED). COUNSEL'S DECLARATION INDICATES THAT DID NOT OCCUR HERE, SO THE DEMURRER AND ITS ASSOCIATED MOTION TO STRIKE ARE ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Sep-27-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANDREY KUKUSHKIN, ANDREY KUKUSHKIN, ANDREY KUKUSHKIN MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

Off calendar. Paragraph 10 of the Gross declaration shows that the parties did not engage in a meaningful meet and confer in compliance with CCP 439. (See Gross Decl., par. 10 ["Defendants indicated that he did not have time to discuss the matter in any depth but that he disagreed that there was any basis for the motion."].) Moreover, the case is set for trial on September 28, 2022, and the motion is untimely per CCP 438(e).

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Plaintiff Andrey Kukushkins Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Portions Of His Opposition To Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, Including Supporting Papers
Off Calendar Sep-14-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANDREY KUKUSHKIN'S Motion To File Under Seal Portions Of His Opposition To Defendants' Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, Including Supporting Papers.

Plaintiff's unopposed motion to seal is granted as follows: The court seals exhibits 24-39 of plaintiff's index of exhibits in opposition to Med Thrive's motion for summary judgment filed on August 19, 2022. There is good cause to seal that private financial information. The court concludes that (1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (See CRC 2.550(d).) The court denies the remainder of the motion to seal as moot. The court did not rely on those documents (Exhibits 40 and 55 of the index) in rendering its decision. Therefore, exhibits 40 and 55 can remain sealed/redacted too without an order of the court.

Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

Atty. MICHAEL E. FOX, Esq. of Kronenberg Law PC (Tel: 510-254-6750) for defendants Med Thrive Cooperative, Inc., Misha Breyburg, and Sasha Plotitsa.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC., MISHA BREYBURG, and SASHA PLOTITSA's Motion For Reconsideration Of April 20 2022 Order On Motions To Compel.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion is denied. Plaintiff claims and possibly might be able to recover damages based on the profit he would have made had defendants not committed alleged wrongful acts during a period when plaintiff alleges he was a minority shareholder and defendants owed him a fiduciary duty. The court has not dismissed plaintiff's claim for these damages. The discovery at issue in this motion is reasonably necessary for plaintiff to be able to prove the amount of these damages.

Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com.

Motion For Reconsideration Of April 20 2022 Order On Motions To Compel
Off Calendar May-04-2022 Continued To Jun-03-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Dismiss Without Prejudice His Derivative Claims (First, Second, Third, Fourth, And Fifth Causes Of Action) From Fifth Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-16-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ANDREY KUKUSHKIN's Motion for Leave TO Dismiss Without Prejudice His Derivative Claims (First, Second, Third, Fourth, And Fifth Causes Of Action) From Fifth Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's "motion for leave to dismiss without prejudice his derivative claims from fifth amended complaint" is granted.

Generally, a plaintiff is free to dismiss any cause of action "with or without prejudice prior to the actual commencement of trial." (CCP 581(c).) Our parties point to only one case providing for court consent to dismissal of shareholder derivative claims - the 62-year-old Ensher v. Ensher, Alexander & Barsoom, Inc. (1960) 187 Cal.App.2d 407, 410.) The theory is that consent is needed because derivative claims belong to the corporation. (Id.) Here, holders of 91% of the corporation's shares were notified of the proposed dismissal and no shareholder indicates any interest in the pursuit of any derivative claim. (Memo. 7:23-8:7.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Aug-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ANDREY KUKUSHKIN'S Motion For Leave To File Fifth Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Andrey Kukushkin's motion for leave to file fifth amended complaint is granted. The motion is supported by the strong policy favoring liberality in amendment of pleadings. If Defendants wish to challenge the viability of the amended complaint, including whether Plaintiff may join individual and derivative claims, whether the direct claims are time-barred, and whether they are barred by judicial estoppel, they may do so by appropriate motion. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."]; see also Swahn Group, Inc. v. Segal (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 831, 843 [if the facts material to a determination of judicial estoppel are undisputed, a question of law is presented that may be decided on demurrer].)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2019 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JEFFREY LINDEN's DEMURRER TO 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Jeffrey Linden's demurrer to the fourth cause of action in plaintiff's fourth amended complaint for unjust enrichment is sustained without leave to amend. "'[A]n action based on an implied-in-fact or quasi-contract cannot lie where there exists between the parties a valid express contract covering the same subject matter.' [Citation.] However, 'restitution may be awarded in lieu of breach of contract damages when the parties had an express contract, but it was procured by fraud or is unenforceable or ineffective for some reason.' [Citation.] Thus, a party to an express contract can assert a claim for restitution by 'alleg[ing in that cause of action] that the express contract is void or was rescinded.'" (Rutherford Holdings, LLC v. Plaza Del Rey (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 221, 231.)

Here, plaintiff expressly alleges in his verified complaint that the nominal defendant Venture Rebel, Inc. and defendant Med Thrive Cooperative, Inc. dba Medithrive, Inc. entered into a written Management Services Agreement "that VENTURE believes is controlling, the terms of which VENTURE acted under and represents MED THRIVE and VENTURE's agreement." (Fourth Amended Complaint 6 & Ex. A; see also id. 69.) He does not allege in the fourth cause of action that the MSA is void or was rescinded, but only, on information and belief, that "Defendants will assert that the MSA is not enforceable as an illegal contract under Federal law or an otherwise defective agreement." (Id. 85.) That allegation is insufficient under controlling authority cited in both of the Court's prior rulings: "Although a plaintiff may plead inconsistent claims that allege both the existence of an enforceable agreement and the absence of an enforceable agreement, that is not what occurred here. Instead, plaintiffs' breach of contract claim pleaded the existence of an unenforceable agreement and their unjust enrichment claim did not deny the existence or enforceability of that agreement. Plaintiffs are therefore precluded from asserting a quasi-contract claim under the theory of unjust enrichment." (Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1389-1390 [affirming order sustaining demurrer to unjust enrichment claim without leave to amend]; accord, Durell v. Sharp Healthcare (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1350, 1370-1371 ["An unjust enrichment theory is inapplicable because [plaintiff] alleges the parties entered into express contracts"]; California Medical Ass'n, Inc. v. Aeta U.S. Healthcare of California, Inc. (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 151, 170-174.)

Plaintiff has already been given two opportunities to amend his complaint to overcome this deficiency, and has failed to do so. Accordingly, the demurrer to this cause of action is sustained without leave to amend.

Counsel for plaintiff is reminded to submit courtesy copies of all of his filed and lodged papers with department 302 as required by Local Rule 2.7(B).

A proposed order is submitted by email. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Strike Fourth Amended Complaint [Ccp 436]
Off Calendar Aug-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC., MISHA BREYBURG, SASHA PLOTITSA's Motion To Strike Fourth Amended Complaint [Ccp 436].

Defendants Med Thrive Cooperative, Inc., Misha Breyburg, and Sasha Plotitsa's motion to strike the fourth amended complaint, joined by defendant Jeffrey Linden, is denied. Although Plaintiff's fourth amended complaint technically was late-filed, the Court's April 23, 2019 order did not specify a deadline for plaintiff to file a further amended complaint, and defendants have not shown they were prejudiced by the 15-day delay. Here, as in Harlan v. Department of Transportation (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 868, "'the delay in filing was brief and inconsequential.'" (Id. at 872-874 [trial court had discretion to accept late-filed second amended complaint without requiring motion for leave to amend].) Defendants' alternative request that the Court condition leave to amend on payment of Defendants' fees for filing the motion to strike is denied.

Counsel for plaintiff is reminded to submit courtesy copies of all of his filed and lodged papers with department 302 as required by Local Rule 2.7(B).

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC., MISHA BREYBURG, SASHA PLOTITSA'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Med Thrive Cooperative, Inc., Misha Breyburg, and Sasha Plotitsa's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for unjust enrichment is sustained with leave to amend. (See Code Civ. Proc. 430.10(e).) Plaintiff has leave to plead specific facts regarding why the Management Services Agreement may be unenforceable, e.g., if the agreement is found to conflict with the Federal Controlled Substances Act. (See Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1389.)

Defendants' demurrer to the first through fourth causes of action on grounds of lack of capacity (Code Civ. Proc. 430.10(b)) is overruled. The court rejects Defendants' argument that Plaintiff cannot prosecute this action because nominal defendant Venture Rebel, Inc. is currently suspended for failing to pay its taxes. "[I]n a stockholders' derivative action, the corporation is forced to be a party because any recovery goes through the corporate channel and thus enhances the stockholders' interest therein. The corporation is not attempting to exercise its rights as a corporation. It is being used as a necessary channel by the shareholders. The books and records of the corporation are in the hands of the mismanaging officers according to plaintiff's complaints and thus the shareholders are not in a position to make a return or compute the franchise tax. In such a case it is not equitable to permit section 23301 of the Revenue & Taxation Code to stand as a shield for protecting allegedly dishonest corporate officials. The corporation is not enjoying any of the privileges accorded to such entities; it is more analogous to the winding up of the business." (Reed v. Norman (1957) 48 Cal.2d 338, 343; see also 9 Witkin, Cal. Proc. 5th Appeal 79 (5th ed. 2008 & 2019 supp.) ["This rule [providing that a suspended corporation may lose the right to invoke legal remedies] will seldom apply to a stockholder's derivative suit."].)

These factors are present in this case. Plaintiff alleges he is a minority shareholder who "has been shut out of the Company" and lacks access to corporate documents. (Third Amended Complaint 6.) In addition, it would be unfair to require Plaintiff-a minority shareholder-to pay all of the corporate taxes to revive the corporation. The court does not find Cohen v. Davis Creek Lumber Co. (1957) 151 Cal.App.2d 420, 427 persuasive, as that Court of Appeal opinion (which has never been followed or cited in California) did not address Reed, which is binding Supreme Court authority. (See Auto Equity Sales, Inc. v. Superior Court (1962) 57 Cal.2d 450, 455 ["Under the doctrine of stare decisis, all tribunals exercising inferior jurisdiction are required to follow decisions of courts exercising superior jurisdiction."].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-04-2019 Continued To May-08-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC., MISHA BREYBURG, SASHA PLOTITSA's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Med Thrive Cooperative, Inc., Misha Breyburg, and Sasha Plotitsa's demurrer to the second, third and fourth causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty, waste, and unjust enrichment of the second amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action and overruled as to the remaining causes of action.

The second amended complaint sufficiently pleads that the individual defendants breached their fiduciary duty to nominal defendant Venture Rebel and engaged in waste of its corporate assets by self-dealing and depletion of Venture's funds with the purpose of benefiting Med Thrive. Those allegations are sufficient to support those causes of action, independent of the terms of the Management Services Agreement or Plaintiff's knowledge of that agreement. Plaintiff's discovery responses regarding that agreement are not properly considered by the Court on demurrer. The Second Amended Complaint is a derivative complaint that does not allege any breach of the Stock Purchase Agreement or the Investor Rights Agreement. Plaintiff Andrey Kukushkin is given 20 days leave to amend the pleading and allege facts, if he can do so in good faith, supporting an alternative theory of recovery based on unjust enrichment. (Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1389-1390 ["a plaintiff may plead inconsistent claims that allege both the existence of an enforceable agreement and the absence of an enforceable agreement.].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-19-2018 Continued To Jan-09-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Finkle, Esq. 510-736-5529 of Finkle Law () for Plaintiff.
Marc Mazer, Esq. 415-421-0730 for Defendant MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC., MISHA BREYBURG, SASHA PLOTITSA .

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant reports there was a settlement with Arman Javid. The Court advises parties it has not been informed of a settlement. The Court denies Defendant's request to continue.

DEFENDANT ARMAN JAVID's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling except for the sentence re: declaration by Defendant's Counsel, Marc Mazer.

Before filing any demurrer, counsel "shall meet and confer in person or by telephone." (CCP 430.41(a) (emphasis added). Counsel's declaration indicates that did not occur here, so the demurrer and its associated motion to strike are ordered OFF CALENDAR. Moreover and in any event, leave to amend is liberally permitted by California's courts, so I GRANT plaintiff's request to file a second amended complaint. (See CCP 473(a), 576; Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 490.) Second Amended complaint to be filed by 10/26/18.

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B.Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
